title: Systems by Security Level
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM FSDJump WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(SystemSecurity_Localised, SystemSecurity, 'Unknown') AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM FSDJump WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY label ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {total} FSD jump(s) across systems of all security levels during the report period. Your security distribution is an honest operational map — a low-security-heavy chart marks a frontier trader, bounty hunter, or someone comfortable with risk; high-security dominance suggests core world work.
details.help: FSDJump SystemSecurity_Localised is the security level of the destination. total drives the title.

tagLines: System security profile compiled from FSD jump telemetry. Operational zone security distribution noted.
Where you jump is where you operate. The security levels tell you what kind of spaces you chose.
High security means protection. Low security means opportunity. Most commanders visit both kinds.
